title_en: Beyond Eye Rubbing: Hidden Risks for Keratoconus
title_zh: 不只是揉眼睛：圓錐角膜的隱藏風險
tldr_en: This study uses genetic data to show that allergies and inflammation increase the risk of keratoconus, an eye disease that changes the cornea's shape. Surprisingly, higher blood sugar levels were found to protect against it.

title_en: Feeling Safe: How to Give Feedback in Class
title_zh: 「請別讓我丟臉」：如何在課堂中給予回饋
tldr_en: A study found that students often feel unsafe when receiving public feedback from classmates after a presentation. To make peer feedback helpful, teachers must create a safe environment where students don't fear embarrassment.

title_en: Sleep Helps Your Brain Predict What's Next
title_zh: 睡眠如何幫助你的大腦預測未來
tldr_en: Scientists found that learning a sequence of images makes your brain anticipate the next one. A nap after learning strengthens this predictive ability and makes the memory more abstract.

title_en: How Bilingual Brains Learn New Patterns
title_zh: 雙語大腦如何學習新模式？
tldr_en: This study found that people with more bilingual experience are faster at learning complex, non-language patterns. Their brains show more efficient communication between different regions after completing the task.

title_en: Our Brains in Sync: The Secret of Musical Duets
title_zh: 我們的大腦在同步：音樂二重奏的秘密
tldr_en: Scientists used wireless brainwave sensors to study pianists playing duets. They found that partners' brains synchronize, and this connection is stronger when their natural playing speeds are similar.

title_en: Eyes on the Music: Visual Gaze and Coordination in Piano Duets
title_zh: 音樂中的注視：鋼琴二重奏中的視覺線索與協調性研究
tldr_en: This research investigates how looking at a partner helps musicians play together more accurately. By studying piano duets, the researcher found that eye contact is especially vital for staying in sync during expressive parts of a performance where the tempo changes.

title_en: Precision Medicine in Keratoconus: Customized vs Conventional Cross-linking Outcomes
title_zh: 圓錐角膜精準醫療：客製化與傳統型交聯術成效對照研究
tldr_en: This randomized trial compared traditional cross-linking with a customized version that targets specific weak areas of the cornea. The customized group showed significantly better corneal flattening and improved visual quality after one year.

title_en: Exploring the Link: Obstructive Sleep Apnea and Keratoconus Risk
title_zh: 探討關聯：阻塞性睡眠呼吸中止症與圓錐角膜之風險研究
tldr_en: This study investigated the prevalence of sleep apnea in people with keratoconus. It found a higher-than-expected overlap between the two conditions, suggesting that systemic inflammation or mechanical factors might connect eye health and sleep quality.

title_en: High Progression Rates of Keratoconus in Children with Down Syndrome
title_zh: 唐氏症兒童圓錐角膜之高惡化率研究
tldr_en: This five-year follow-up study investigated the development of keratoconus in children with Down syndrome. It found that the disease progresses much more rapidly and frequently in this population, requiring more aggressive monitoring.

title_en: Pregnancy and Corneal Stability: Assessing Progression in Keratoconus Patients
title_zh: 懷孕與角膜穩定性：評估圓錐角膜患者之病情進展研究
tldr_en: This study explored whether pregnancy triggers progression in women with keratoconus. It found significant evidence that hormonal changes during pregnancy can lead to corneal steepening and decreased mechanical stiffness.

title_en: Predicting the Path: Machine Learning for Keratoconus Progression
title_zh: 預測病程：利用機器學習預測圓錐角膜之進展研究
tldr_en: Researchers developed a machine learning model to predict which keratoconus patients are most likely to get worse over time. By analyzing initial eye scans, the model achieved high accuracy in identifying high-risk cases before they lost vision.

title_en: Clearing the Haze: Measuring Corneal Clarity After Cross-linking
title_zh: 撥雲見日：衡量交聯術後角膜清晰度之變化研究
tldr_en: This study monitored corneal haze, or cloudiness, after patients received accelerated cross-linking. It found that while haze peaks shortly after surgery, most patients' eyes return to their original clarity within twelve months.

title_en: Beyond Displeasure: The Physiological Impact of Disliked Music
title_zh: 不只是不悅：厭惡音樂對人體生理之影響研究
tldr_en: This study investigates how our bodies react to music we dislike compared to music we enjoy. Researchers found that listening to hated songs actually triggers a physical stress response, increasing heart rate and skin sweat, proving that bad music is a biological stressor.
